title: Late Submission and Asking the Teacher for Extension
title_zh: 遲交作業與向老師請求延期

all.en: Kevin knocked softly on the door of Ms. Adams's office after the last bell of the day.
all.zh: 凱文在當天最後一節鐘聲響後輕輕敲了亞當斯老師辦公室的門。

all.en: He had missed the deadline for his English literature essay by two whole days.
all.zh: 他的英國文學論文已經錯過截止日期整整兩天了。

all.en: Ms. Adams invited him to sit down and asked him to explain what had happened.
all.zh: 亞當斯老師請他坐下，並請他解釋發生了什麼事。

all.en: Kevin nervously admitted that his grandmother had been hospitalized over the weekend.
all.zh: 凱文緊張地承認他的祖母週末住院了。

all.en: He had been helping his family at the hospital and could not focus on writing.
all.zh: 他一直在醫院幫忙家人，無法專心寫作。

all.en: Ms. Adams listened patiently and expressed her sympathy for his difficult situation.
all.zh: 亞當斯老師耐心傾聽，對他困難的處境表達同情。

all.en: She agreed to grant him a three-day extension without lowering his final grade.
all.zh: 她同意給他三天的延期，並且不會降低他的最終成績。

all.en: However, she reminded him to communicate earlier next time if any problems occurred.
all.zh: 然而，她提醒他下次如果發生任何問題要更早溝通。

all.en: Kevin thanked her sincerely and promised to submit a high-quality essay on time.
all.zh: 凱文真誠地感謝她，並承諾會準時繳交一份高品質的論文。

all.en: He left the office feeling relieved and grateful for her kindness and understanding.
all.zh: 他離開辦公室時感到鬆了一口氣，並對她的善意和理解心存感激。

all.en: That night, he stayed up late polishing his essay, determined not to disappoint her.
all.zh: 那天晚上，他熬夜潤飾他的論文，決心不讓她失望。

all.en: The next morning, Kevin walked into class with the printed essay tucked under his arm.
all.zh: 隔天早上，凱文把列印好的論文夾在腋下走進教室。

all.en: He placed it gently on Ms. Adams's desk and thanked her once again for the chance.
all.zh: 他輕輕地把論文放在亞當斯老師的桌上，再次感謝她給予的機會。

all.en: Ms. Adams skimmed the first paragraph and gave him an approving nod from her chair.
all.zh: 亞當斯老師翻看了第一段，從椅子上對他點頭表示認可。

all.en: During lunch, Kevin called the hospital to check on his grandmother's recovery progress.
all.zh: 午餐時間，凱文打電話到醫院關心祖母的康復狀況。

all.en: The nurse cheerfully told him that she had eaten a full meal that morning.
all.zh: 護理師開心地告訴他，祖母早上吃了一整份餐點。

all.en: A week later, Ms. Adams returned the essay with a bright red A written on top.
all.zh: 一週後，亞當斯老師發回論文，上頭寫著一個鮮紅的 A 字。

all.en: She added a comment praising the depth of his analysis and the honesty of his voice.
all.zh: 她寫下評語，稱讚他分析的深度與聲音的真誠。

all.en: Kevin showed the grade to his mother, who hugged him tightly in the kitchen.
all.zh: 凱文把成績拿給母親看，她在廚房裡緊緊抱住了他。

all.en: From that day on, he started planning assignments at least one week ahead of time.
all.zh: 從那天起，他開始至少提前一週規劃每份作業。

all.en: He also wrote a thank-you card and slipped it into Ms. Adams's mailbox on Friday.
all.zh: 他還寫了一張感謝卡，週五悄悄塞進亞當斯老師的信箱裡。

all.en: The following Monday, Ms. Adams stopped him in the hallway with a warm smile.
all.zh: 接下來的星期一，亞當斯老師在走廊上叫住他，露出溫暖的微笑。

all.en: She invited him to read his essay aloud during the next literature club meeting.
all.zh: 她邀請他在下次文學社的聚會上朗讀自己的論文。

all.en: Kevin hesitated at first, but the thought of his grandmother gave him courage.
all.zh: 凱文起初有些猶豫，但想到祖母便鼓起了勇氣。

all.en: He practiced reading aloud every evening, paying attention to pace and emotion.
all.zh: 他每天晚上都練習朗讀，特別注意語速和情感。

all.en: On Thursday, the club members listened in silence as he shared his honest words.
all.zh: 星期四，社團成員們靜靜聆聽他分享真誠的文字。

all.en: Several classmates approached him afterward, thanking him for his moving story.
all.zh: 幾位同學會後上前感謝他這個動人的故事。

all.en: One girl said her own mother had been ill, and his words gave her hope.
all.zh: 一位女孩說自己的母親也曾生病，他的話給了她希望。

all.en: Kevin began volunteering at the hospital every Saturday morning with great enthusiasm.
all.zh: 凱文開始每週六上午滿懷熱忱地到醫院當志工。

all.en: His grandmother recovered fully and baked him a cake on his eighteenth birthday.
all.zh: 祖母完全康復，並在他十八歲生日時為他烤了一個蛋糕。

all.en: Years later, Kevin became a teacher who always reminded students to communicate early.
all.zh: 多年後，凱文成為一位老師，總是提醒學生要儘早溝通。

all.en: In his first year of teaching, Kevin assigned an essay about overcoming personal struggles.
all.zh: 在教書的第一年，凱文指派了一篇關於克服個人困境的論文作業。

all.en: He shared his own story about his grandmother and the late essay on the first day.
all.zh: 他在第一天就分享了自己關於祖母與遲交論文的故事。

all.en: His students listened carefully, surprised that a teacher would admit such a mistake.
all.zh: 學生們仔細聆聽，驚訝於一位老師竟願意坦承這樣的過失。

all.en: One quiet boy came to his desk after class and asked for advice about his father.
all.zh: 一位安靜的男孩下課後走到他的桌前，請教關於父親的事。

all.en: Kevin invited him to sit down and listened just as Ms. Adams had once listened to him.
all.zh: 凱文請他坐下，就像當年亞當斯老師傾聽自己一樣專注聆聽。

all.en: He gave the boy a short extension and reminded him to communicate earlier next time.
all.zh: 他給了男孩一個短暫的延期，並提醒他下次要更早溝通。

all.en: Throughout that semester, Kevin kept a small note from Ms. Adams pinned above his desk.
all.zh: 整個學期，凱文都把亞當斯老師的一張小紙條釘在桌子上方。

all.en: At the end of the year, the quiet boy submitted an essay full of honest emotion.
all.zh: 學年結束時，那位安靜的男孩交出了一篇充滿真摯情感的論文。

all.en: Kevin felt tears in his eyes as he read the heartfelt final paragraph aloud.
all.zh: 凱文朗讀那段真誠的結尾段落時，眼眶不禁泛起淚水。

all.en: He realized that one kind teacher had quietly shaped two whole generations of writers.
all.zh: 他體會到，一位善良的老師竟悄悄塑造了兩個世代的寫作者。

all.en: On graduation day, the quiet boy hugged Kevin and whispered a heartfelt thank-you.
all.zh: 畢業典禮當天，那位安靜的男孩擁抱凱文，輕聲說出真摯的感謝。

all.en: He confessed that Kevin's patience had saved him from giving up on school entirely.
all.zh: 他坦言，是凱文的耐心讓他沒有徹底放棄學業。

all.en: Kevin felt the weight of Ms. Adams's lesson passing on to yet another generation.
all.zh: 凱文感受到亞當斯老師的教導正悄悄傳遞給又一個世代。

all.en: Years later, that same quiet boy returned to the school as a new English teacher.
all.zh: 多年後，那位安靜的男孩回到這所學校，成為一位新進的英文教師。

all.en: He carried his own thank-you card and placed it carefully above his classroom desk.
all.zh: 他帶著自己的感謝卡，小心翼翼地貼在教室桌子上方。

all.en: Kevin attended the welcome ceremony and shook the young man's hand with pride.
all.zh: 凱文出席了歡迎儀式，驕傲地與這位年輕人握手致意。

all.en: They walked the school corridors together, sharing stories of every difficult student.
all.zh: 他們一起走過校園走廊，分享關於每位艱辛學生的故事。

all.en: Kevin told him that real teaching meant listening longer than you ever spoke.
all.zh: 凱文告訴他，真正的教學是傾聽的時間比說話的時間更長。

all.en: They promised to meet for tea every month and to mentor each other for life.
all.zh: 他們約定每月一同喝茶，並終身互相指導扶持。

all.en: Kevin walked home knowing kindness had quietly become the longest chain he ever built.
all.zh: 凱文走回家時明白，善意已默默成為他建立過最長的一條鏈。

all.en: On their next tea meeting, Kevin and his old mentor planned a small mentorship circle for new teachers.
all.zh: 下次喝茶聚會時，凱文和他從前的恩師計畫為新進老師成立一個小型師徒圈。

all.en: They invited eight teachers from three nearby schools and met every second Saturday afternoon at a cozy café.
all.zh: 他們邀請了附近三所學校共八位老師，每隔一週的週六下午在一間溫馨的咖啡館聚會。

all.en: Each meeting centered on one real student case, focusing on empathy and creative classroom solutions.
all.zh: 每次聚會都圍繞一個真實的學生案例，重點放在同理心和有創意的課堂解決方案。

all.en: Younger teachers gained confidence quickly, supported by warm advice instead of harsh professional criticism.
all.zh: 年輕老師很快建立起自信，因為得到的是溫暖的建議，而不是嚴厲的職業批評。

all.en: Kevin began writing a small book about kind classroom leadership, drawn from years of real student stories.
all.zh: 凱文開始撰寫一本關於溫柔課堂領導的小書，內容取材自多年來真實的學生故事。

all.en: His mentor wrote the foreword, sharing the story of the late submission that had started everything.
all.zh: 他的恩師撰寫了序言，分享那次遲交作業如何成為一切的起點。

all.en: The book sold steadily in local educational circles and reached schools far beyond their original city.
all.zh: 這本書在當地教育圈裡銷量穩定，並流傳到他們原本城市以外的學校。

all.en: One reader wrote that the book had saved his teaching career during his most discouraging semester.
all.zh: 一位讀者寫信說，這本書在他最沮喪的學期裡，挽救了他的教師生涯。

all.en: Kevin framed the letter and placed it beside the small handwritten note from his oldest mentor's tea cup.
all.zh: 凱文把那封信裱框，放在他最敬愛的恩師茶杯旁那張手寫小紙條的旁邊。

all.en: He realized that listening longer than speaking had truly become the cornerstone of everything he taught.
all.zh: 他體會到傾聽得比說話更久，已真正成為他所教導一切的基石。

all.en: The following spring, Kevin received a teaching excellence award from the regional education committee.
all.zh: 隔年春天，凱文獲頒區域教育委員會頒發的卓越教學獎。

all.en: At the ceremony, he asked Ms. Adams to sit beside him on the front row as his honored guest.
all.zh: 在頒獎典禮上，他請亞當斯老師作為他的尊貴嘉賓坐在前排陪同。

all.en: She had grown silver-haired but still wore the same gentle smile he remembered from his youth.
all.zh: 她已是滿頭銀髮，但仍帶著他年輕時所記得的那抹溫柔微笑。

all.en: Kevin dedicated his award to her, recalling the rainy day he had knocked on her office door.
all.zh: 凱文把這份獎項獻給她，回憶起當年下雨天他敲響她辦公室門的那一刻。

all.en: She nodded slowly, her eyes shining, as the entire audience rose to applaud them both warmly.
all.zh: 她緩緩點頭，雙眼閃著淚光，全場觀眾起立熱情地為他們兩人鼓掌。

all.en: After the ceremony, they walked together to a quiet bench beneath an old cherry blossom tree.
all.zh: 典禮結束後，他們一起走到一棵老櫻花樹下的安靜長椅旁。

all.en: Ms. Adams confessed that his thank-you card had remained on her desk for over thirty years.
all.zh: 亞當斯老師坦言，他的感謝卡在她的桌上已經放了超過三十年。

all.en: Kevin laughed and pulled out his own well-worn note from her, kept inside his old wallet.
all.zh: 凱文笑了，從他那本舊皮夾裡掏出她當年寫給他的、早已磨損的小紙條。

all.en: They sat in companionable silence as cherry petals drifted slowly around their shoulders.
all.zh: 他們在友伴般的靜默中坐著，櫻花花瓣緩緩飄落在兩人的肩頭。

all.en: Kevin understood that a single moment of patience had quietly multiplied across countless unseen lives.
all.zh: 凱文明白，一個短暫的耐心瞬間，已悄悄地在無數看不見的生命中倍增綻放。

all.en: The next semester, Kevin invited Ms. Adams to co-host a small workshop for first-year university students.
all.zh: 下個學期，凱文邀請亞當斯老師共同主持一場大一新生的小型工作坊。

all.en: She agreed warmly, saying that mentoring alongside her former student would close a beautiful lifelong circle.
all.zh: 她溫暖地答應，說能與從前的學生一同指導，會圓滿一個美麗的終生圓圈。

all.en: Twenty nervous students attended the first workshop, holding crumpled drafts and quiet hopeful smiles.
all.zh: 二十位緊張的學生來到第一場工作坊，手裡握著皺巴巴的草稿和靜謐期待的微笑。

all.en: Kevin opened by sharing the rainy day he had once knocked on Ms. Adams's office door alone.
all.zh: 凱文以那個獨自敲響亞當斯老師辦公室門的雨天故事作為開場。

all.en: Ms. Adams added her own perspective, describing how courage often arrived quietly through small honest requests.
all.zh: 亞當斯老師補充了自己的觀點，講述勇氣往往透過小小的真誠請求悄然來臨。

all.en: Students wrote letters to their future selves and sealed them inside envelopes for next year's opening.
all.zh: 學生們寫信給未來的自己，並把信封封好留待明年開啟。

all.en: Kevin promised to safely keep every envelope inside the same wooden box that held his thank-you card.
all.zh: 凱文承諾把每一封信安全地保存在那個收藏他感謝卡的木盒裡。

all.en: Ms. Adams smiled gently and whispered that teaching had always been a quiet conversation across time.
all.zh: 亞當斯老師溫和微笑，輕聲說教學從來就是一場跨越時間的安靜對話。

all.en: After the workshop, several students lingered to ask whether such kindness could really change real lives.
all.zh: 工作坊結束後，幾位學生留下來，詢問這樣的善意是否真能改變真實的生命。

all.en: Kevin gestured toward Ms. Adams beside him and answered that the proof was standing right next to him.
all.zh: 凱文指向身旁的亞當斯老師，回答證據就站在他身邊。

all.en: After the workshop, Kevin and Ms. Adams ducked into a small noodle shop two streets away to escape the autumn rain.
all.zh: 工作坊結束後，凱文和亞當斯老師躲進兩條街外的一家小麵店，避開秋雨。

all.en: Ms. Adams insisted on paying for both bowls, claiming retired teachers had to spoil their former students at least once.
all.zh: 亞當斯老師堅持要請兩碗麵，說退休老師至少要寵以前的學生一次。

all.en: Kevin laughed and quietly tipped the noodle shop owner twice the usual amount when she went to the restroom.
all.zh: 凱文笑了，在她去洗手間時，悄悄給了麵店老闆雙倍的小費。

all.en: On the train home, Kevin opened the wooden box and added Ms. Adams's used chopsticks paper sleeve as a quiet souvenir.
all.zh: 回家的火車上，凱文打開那個木盒，把亞當斯老師用過的免洗筷紙套放進去當作安靜的紀念。

all.en: The next morning, three students from the workshop emailed thank-you notes, each one mentioning Ms. Adams's gentle perspective on courage.
all.zh: 隔天早晨，工作坊有三位學生寄來感謝信，每一封都提到亞當斯老師對勇氣的溫柔詮釋。

all.en: Kevin printed the messages and mailed them to Ms. Adams in a single envelope with a tiny pressed cherry blossom inside.
all.zh: 凱文把這些訊息列印出來，連同一片壓乾的小櫻花一起放進一個信封，寄給亞當斯老師。

all.en: She called him that evening to say she had cried softly in her kitchen while reading every single word.
all.zh: 她那晚打電話來說，她在廚房裡讀每一個字時，輕輕地哭了出來。

all.en: Kevin decided to invite Ms. Adams to co-host an annual workshop every spring, framing it as their quiet shared ritual.
all.zh: 凱文決定邀請亞當斯老師每年春天共同主持工作坊，把它視為他們之間安靜共享的儀式。

all.en: She accepted gladly, saying she now had a reason to keep her old teaching shoes polished and ready by the door.
all.zh: 她欣然答應，說她現在有理由把那雙舊教學鞋擦亮，並放在門邊隨時備用。

all.en: Before bed that night, Kevin wrote in his journal that some kindnesses came back every year, like the cherry blossoms outside her old office window.
all.zh: 那晚睡前，凱文在日記裡寫下：有些善意會年復一年地回來，就像她舊辦公室窗外的櫻花。
